prose: Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT with the B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional-basis pair (tier standard, density-fit approximation) in PySCF, gas phase, at charge 0 multiplicity 1 (neutral species, with cation at +1/doublet and anion at −1/doublet for Fukui state calculations). Geometry: user-supplied XYZ file, single-point (not relaxed). All three SCF states (neutral, N+1, N−1) converged. Charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). Most electrophilic site (f⁺): ortho carbon (atom 4), f⁺ = 0.49641782. Most nucleophilic site (f⁻): meta carbon (atom 5), f⁻ = 0.1072882. Dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) at ortho carbon = 0.51070986, indicating strong electrophilic activation. Full atom-by-atom Fukui indices and dual descriptors reported in results above. Command: chemkit fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz. Live log: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260702-221839_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260702-221844.out.
